title: Montgomery Career Center (Comprehensive)
text: Montgomery Career Center (Comprehensive). Category: career_center. Type: comprehensive. Services: ["Job search assistance", "Resume writing", "Interview preparation", "Career counseling", "Workshops", "Computer access", "WIOA enrollment", "Veterans services"]. Eligibility: Open to all Montgomery residents, no appointment needed. Address: 1060 East South Boulevard, Montgomery, AL 36116. Phone: 334-286-1746. Hours: Mon-Fri 8am-5pm. Ask for referrals to specialized programs. WIOA funding can cover training costs.

title: Montgomery Regional Workforce Training Center (MRWTC)
text: Montgomery Regional Workforce Training Center (MRWTC). Category: training. Type: healthcare. Services: ["CNA certification", "Phlebotomy", "EKG Technician", "Patient Care Technician", "CDL training", "Welding", "Electrical"]. Eligibility: Montgomery area residents, some programs require GED/diploma. Address: 1095 East South Boulevard, Montgomery, AL 36116. Phone: 334-420-4560. Healthcare re-certification programs available. WIOA funding accepted.

title: Alabama Industrial Development Training (AIDT)
text: Alabama Industrial Development Training (AIDT). Category: training. Type: manufacturing. Services: ["Manufacturing skills", "Industrial maintenance", "Robotics", "Lean manufacturing", "Safety certifications"]. Eligibility: Alabama residents. Address: One Technology Court, Montgomery, AL 36116. Phone: 334-280-4400. Free training for qualifying employers. Partners with Hyundai, GE Aviation.

title: Trenholm State Community College
text: Trenholm State Community College. Category: training. Type: general. Services: ["GED preparation", "Nursing", "Automotive technology", "Business administration", "Computer science", "Cosmetology"]. Eligibility: Open enrollment. Address: 1225 Air Base Boulevard, Montgomery, AL 36108. Phone: 334-420-4200. Pell Grant eligible. Short-term workforce certificates available.

title: MPACT, Montgomery Preparedness and Career Training
text: MPACT, Montgomery Preparedness and Career Training. Category: training. Type: reentry. Services: ["Job readiness", "Life skills", "Mentoring", "Employer connections", "Support services"]. Eligibility: Returning citizens, justice-involved individuals. Address: 215 Clayton Street, Montgomery, AL 36104. Phone: 334-625-4700. Specifically designed for formerly incarcerated individuals re-entering the workforce.

title: Alabama DHR Child Care Subsidy Program
text: Alabama DHR Child Care Subsidy Program. Category: childcare. Type: subsidy. Services: ["Childcare payment assistance", "Provider referrals"]. Eligibility: Income-eligible families, must be working or in approved training. Address: 3060 Mobile Highway, Montgomery, AL 36108. Phone: 334-242-1310. Apply through local DHR office. Processing takes 2-4 weeks. Covers licensed centers and registered homes.

title: Head Start / Early Head Start, Montgomery
text: Head Start / Early Head Start, Montgomery. Category: childcare. Type: head_start. Services: ["Free preschool", "Health screenings", "Nutrition services", "Parent education", "Disability services"]. Eligibility: Families at or below federal poverty level, children ages 0-5. Address: 1407 Federal Drive, Montgomery, AL 36107. Phone: 334-262-4734. Free program. Also serves pregnant women. Apply early, waitlists common.

title: Family Guidance Center of Alabama
text: Family Guidance Center of Alabama. Category: childcare. Type: family_services. Services: ["Childcare referrals", "Parenting classes", "Counseling", "Crisis intervention", "Substance abuse treatment"]. Address: 925 Forest Avenue, Montgomery, AL 36106. Phone: 334-270-4100. Sliding scale fees. Walk-ins accepted for crisis services.

title: Montgomery OneAlabama, 2-1-1 Helpline
text: Montgomery OneAlabama, 2-1-1 Helpline. Category: social_service. Type: referral. Services: ["Resource referrals", "Emergency assistance", "Food assistance", "Housing assistance", "Utility assistance"]. Address: 600 South Court Street, Montgomery, AL 36104. Phone: 211. Call 2-1-1 for immediate connection to local resources. Available 24/7.

title: Alabama JOBS Program (DHR)
text: Alabama JOBS Program (DHR). Category: social_service. Type: employment. Services: ["Job search assistance", "Transportation assistance", "Work experience placements", "Education support"]. Eligibility: TANF recipients. Address: 3060 Mobile Highway, Montgomery, AL 36108. Phone: 334-242-1310. Mandatory for able-bodied TANF recipients. Provides transportation vouchers.

title: Montgomery Area Transit System (MATS), The M
text: Montgomery Area Transit System (MATS), The M. Category: social_service. Type: transportation. Services: ["Fixed-route bus service", "Paratransit", "Monthly passes"]. Address: 2318 West Fairview Avenue, Montgomery, AL 36108. Phone: 334-262-7321. 31-day pass available at MATS office. No Sunday service. Download TransLoc app for real-time tracking.

title: Salvation Army, Montgomery
text: Salvation Army, Montgomery. Category: social_service. Type: emergency. Services: ["Emergency shelter", "Food pantry", "Utility assistance", "Clothing", "Case management"]. Address: 911 South Court Street, Montgomery, AL 36104. Phone: 334-265-4681. Walk-in for food pantry. Shelter requires intake appointment.

title: GreenPath Financial Wellness
text: GreenPath Financial Wellness. Category: social_service. Type: credit_counseling. Services: ["Credit counseling", "Debt management plans", "Budget coaching", "Student loan counseling", "Housing counseling"]. Eligibility: Open to all, free initial consultation. Address: 36 S Court Street, Suite 400, Montgomery, AL 36104. Phone: 800-550-1961. HUD-approved nonprofit. Free credit report review. Debt management plans can reduce interest rates and consolidate payments.

title: Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Central Alabama
text: Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Central Alabama. Category: social_service. Type: credit_counseling. Services: ["Credit counseling", "Debt management", "Financial education workshops", "Pre-purchase housing counseling", "Bankruptcy counseling"]. Eligibility: Open to all Alabama residents. Address: 600 South Court Street, Suite 300, Montgomery, AL 36104. Phone: 334-265-8545. Nonprofit. Sliding scale fees. Walk-ins welcome for initial consultation. Ask about free financial literacy workshops.

title: Action Plan: No Daytime Childcare
text: Connect client with DHR childcare voucher program and local Head Start centers. Verify eligibility for CCDF subsidies and assist with application.

title: Action Plan: No Evening Childcare
text: Identify licensed evening care providers in Montgomery area. Explore family support networks and employer-sponsored backup care options.

title: Action Plan: No Infant Care Available
text: Check waitlists at licensed infant care centers. Connect with Early Head Start and home-based care provider networks for immediate openings.

title: Action Plan: Unaware of Childcare Subsidies
text: Educate client on CCDF and DHR childcare voucher eligibility. Walk through application process and provide contact info for local DHR office.

title: Action Plan: Childcare Unaffordable
text: Calculate childcare cost vs. income ratio. Apply for sliding-scale fee programs and explore employer childcare benefits or co-op arrangements.

title: Action Plan: No Personal Vehicle
text: Map transit routes from client's home to potential employers. Connect with vehicle assistance programs and explore carpool matching services.

title: Action Plan: No Public Transit Access
text: Identify alternative transportation: vanpools, ride-share programs, employer shuttles. Consider relocation assistance if housing is flexible.

title: Action Plan: No Night Shift Transportation
text: Check employer willingness to adjust shifts. Explore ride-share stipends, employer shuttle programs, and bicycle commuting for short distances.

title: Action Plan: Unreliable Transportation
text: Connect with auto repair assistance programs. Establish backup transportation plan with transit, carpool, or ride-share as contingency.

title: Action Plan: Transportation Cost Too High
text: Calculate commute cost vs. income. Apply for transit pass subsidies, explore employer transit benefits, and consider closer employment options.

title: Action Plan: Low Credit Score
text: Pull free credit report to identify specific issues. Create a credit improvement plan targeting highest-impact items first. Connect with credit counseling.

title: Action Plan: No Credit History
text: Open a secured credit card or credit-builder loan. Enroll in rent reporting programs to build credit history from existing payments.

title: Action Plan: High Debt Burden
text: Create debt inventory and prioritize by interest rate. Explore debt consolidation, income-driven repayment plans, and negotiate with creditors.

title: Action Plan: Accounts in Collections
text: Verify debt validity under FDCPA. Negotiate pay-for-delete agreements where possible. Connect with legal aid for disputed or invalid debts.

title: Action Plan: No Bank Account
text: Identify second-chance banking programs at local credit unions. Assist with opening a basic checking account to enable direct deposit.

title: Action Plan: Unstable Housing
text: Connect with emergency housing programs and shelters. Apply for rapid rehousing assistance and establish stable address for employment applications.

title: Action Plan: Housing Unaffordable
text: Apply for Section 8 or public housing waitlists. Explore shared housing options and negotiate with landlord for reduced rent or payment plan.

title: Action Plan: Eviction on Record
text: Connect with legal aid to review eviction record for expungement eligibility. Identify landlords who accept applicants with eviction history.

title: Action Plan: No Health Insurance
text: Screen for Medicaid eligibility and assist with enrollment. Check marketplace plans during open enrollment. Connect with community health centers for immediate care.

title: Action Plan: Physical Disability
text: Document accommodation needs under ADA. Connect with vocational rehabilitation services for job placement support and workplace modification assistance.

title: Action Plan: Mental Health Challenges
text: Connect with mental health providers accepting Medicaid or sliding-scale fees. Explore Employee Assistance Programs and workplace accommodation options.

title: Action Plan: Substance Use Disorder
text: Connect with SAMHSA treatment locator and local recovery programs. Support treatment-compatible work schedules and sober living arrangements.

title: Action Plan: No Professional Certification
text: Identify relevant certifications for target industry. Connect with WIOA-funded training programs and scholarship opportunities for certification exams.

title: Action Plan: Skills Gap
text: Conduct skills assessment to identify specific gaps. Enroll in targeted short-term training at community college or workforce development center.

title: Action Plan: No GED or High School Diploma
text: Enroll in GED preparation program at local adult education center. Explore employer-sponsored GED programs and online preparation resources.

title: Action Plan: Outdated Job Skills
text: Assess transferable skills and identify in-demand fields. Connect with retraining programs and apprenticeship opportunities in growing industries.

title: Action Plan: Criminal Record
text: Research ban-the-box employers in Montgomery area. Explore expungement eligibility and connect with reentry employment programs.

title: Action Plan: Background Check Barrier
text: Identify fair-chance employers who evaluate candidates individually. Prepare disclosure statement and gather character references for applications.

title: Action Plan: Reentry from Incarceration
text: Connect with reentry programs for job readiness training. Assist with ID documents, resume development, and transitional employment placement.

title: Action Plan: Extended Employment Gap
text: Develop narrative explaining gap positively. Fill with volunteer work, training, or freelance experience. Practice interview responses for gap questions.

title: Action Plan: Low Income / Underemployment
text: Identify higher-paying opportunities matching client skills. Connect with career advancement programs and negotiate for raises or promotions at current employer.

title: Action Plan: Limited Work Hours Available
text: Address root constraints first (childcare, transportation). Explore remote work options and employers offering flexible scheduling.

title: Action Plan: Job Instability
text: Identify and address root-cause barriers creating instability. Build emergency fund plan and develop retention strategies with employer support.
